context: Barry PhD Paper 1 AAAJ-style Word rebuild
symptom: Blank rebuild via Word FormattedText preserved tables, inline shapes, and sections but changed PDF pagination from 66 to 80 pages.
cause: FormattedText transfer did not preserve enough of the source template/layout context for exact pagination.
recovery: For exact baseline reproduction, use an empty Word document plus Word original-format insertion, then validate PDF page count. For stable EXP, extract explicit style rules and test on new practice content.

context: Barry PhD Paper 1 AAAJ-style Word rebuild
symptom: A rebuild matched 66 source PDF pages, tables, images, and sections, but page-level visual comparison showed shifted content on pages such as 22 and 23.
cause: The rebuild preserved object counts and page count but not the full template context needed for identical page boundaries.
recovery: Render source and rebuilt PDFs page by page and require visual comparison to pass. Exact baseline reproduction passed only after creating the blank rebuild inside the source template context.

context: Barry PhD Paper Word draft after Sakura/librarian citation repair
symptom: The accepted Step 10 citation workflow output had all 357 paragraphs in Normal style, leaving title, headings, abstract, keywords, captions, body, and references visually flat despite the text structure being present.
cause: Citation repair workflow preserved text and references but did not preserve or reconstruct AAAJ visual style hierarchy.
recovery: Create a separate editing copy, then apply an AAAJ visual formatting pass: A4 page setup, Times New Roman, centered title/subtitle, Abstract/Keywords labels, centered main headings, body double spacing with first-line indent, captions, and hanging-indent references. Validate in desktop Word and export PDF.

context: Barry PhD Paper Word draft after Sakura/librarian citation repair
symptom: In-text citations rendered as blue hyperlink-style text in Word, making the paper look less like a clean journal manuscript.
cause: Citation repair retained hyperlink formatting in runs even after text-level citation validation passed.
recovery: During visual formatting, force citation/link runs to Times New Roman black text with no underline unless Barry explicitly wants clickable citation styling preserved visually.
